differential_diagnoses: Supraventricular tachycardia (atrial flutter or atrial fibrillation)
Digoxin toxicity (if applicable, or medication-related toxicity)
next_steps: Immediate transfer to the emergency department for cardiac monitoring and stabilization
Urgent ICD interrogation to assess for rhythm storage and therapy delivery
Point-of-care cardiac ultrasound to evaluate ventricular function and mitral valve integrity
Stat laboratory evaluation including serum electrolytes, troponin, and repeat NT-proBNP
Hemodynamic stabilization with cautious fluid resuscitation or vasopressors as indicated
Consultation with electrophysiology service for potential management of sustained tachycardia

differential_diagnoses: Digitalis toxicity (presumed due to symptoms of anorexia, nausea, and visual disturbance)
next_steps: Transfer to the emergency department for continuous cardiac monitoring
Obtain a 12-lead ECG to determine the mechanism of tachycardia
Urgent ICD interrogation and review of stored event logs
Stat serum electrolytes (potassium, magnesium) and cardiac biomarkers (troponin)
Medication reconciliation and review for hidden digoxin sources (e.g., herbal supplements)
Obtain TSH and free T4 levels
Perform point-of-care cardiac ultrasound to evaluate ventricular function and valve integrity
